patterns.name: Valid products incorrectly marked as unavailable
patterns.description: The search engine returns no hits or filters out hits with a confidence score that is too strict, so even clearly matching items (e.g., "Портативна поїлка‑бутилка", "Шампунь GILL'S") are reported as missing.
patterns.fix: Lower the confidence‑score threshold to 0.4 and add a fallback that returns the top‑3 results regardless of score when no item passes the threshold.

patterns.name: Price‑filter over‑rejects items
patterns.description: The price‑filter logic treats any price > requested max as a hard reject, ignoring that the catalogue contains items exactly at the limit (e.g., 100 грн).
patterns.fix: Change the price check to `price <= max_price` and allow a small tolerance (±5 грн).

patterns.name: Chain turns request clarification instead of using prior context
patterns.description: When a query is part of a chain, the analyzer resets the intent instead of inheriting the previous product‑type context, causing generic clarification prompts.
patterns.fix: Propagate the previous intent (e.g., `last_product_category`) into the current turn and skip the clarification step if a matching product exists.

analysis.top_recommendation: Lower the confidence threshold in retrieval.py and add a fallback to return the top‑3 matches; this alone will resolve the majority of "product not found" failures across both single and chain queries.

selfcorrect_patch.new_text: if confidence < 0.4: return None  # lowered threshold to accept more relevant matches

patch.new_text: if confidence < 0.4: return None  # lowered threshold to accept more relevant matches
